name: White Plains Beach
tagline: A former military beach now open to the public — wide sand, consistent beginner waves, and no Waikiki crowds.
parking: Large free lot; ample parking even on weekends
bestTime: Morning for glassy surf conditions; year-round for consistent small waves
talkStory: White Plains was a restricted military beach at Barbers Point Naval Air Station until the base closed in 1999 and public access was restored. The beach breaks here produce small, forgiving waves that peel consistently — locals consider it the best learn-to-surf spot on Oahu, without the chaos and rental-board traffic of Waikiki. Board rentals and lessons are available right on the sand. The beach is wide with soft sand and rarely crowded even on weekends thanks to the Ewa location most tourists overlook.
